gauge.name: Nancy Creek at Johnson Ferry Road near Chamblee

impacts.statement: Record flooding continues upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. A large portion of the Marist High School campus will be flooded...including parking lots...the baseball and football fields...and the parking garage under the gym. Major flooding will continue downstream in Fulton County to Rickenbacker Drive. Many homes on East Powderhorn Road...Hitching Post Trail...Pine Forest Road...Valley Brook Drive and Valley Green Drive will flood. The impacts will be similar to September 2004.

Record flooding occurs upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. A large portion of the Marist High School campus will be flooded...including parking lots...the baseball and football fields...and the parking garage under the gym. Major flooding will continue downstream in Fulton County to Rickenbacker Drive. Many homes on East Powderhorn Road...Hitching Post Trail...Pine Forest Road...Valley Brook Drive and Valley Green Drive will flood. The impacts will be similar to September 2009.

Moderate flooding expands into the woodlands and athletic fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. A large portion of the Marist High School campus will be flooded...including parking lots...baseball fields...and the parking garage under the gym. Major flooding will develop downstream in Fulton County to Rickenbacker Drive. Many homes on East Powderhorn Road...Hitching Post Trail...Pine Forest Road...Valley Brook Drive and Valley Green Drive will be flooded.

Moderate flooding begins in the woodlands and athletic fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. The pedestrian and vehicular bridges to the visitor parking lots on the Marist High School campus will be inundated. The student parking lot and baseball fields will be flooded with 1 to 3 feet of water. Backed up storm drains will likely flood the parking garage under the gym. Moderate flooding continues downstream to Rickenbacker Drive. Some homes will begin to flood near the creek.

Minor flooding expands further into the woodlands and athletic fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. The water will be around 3 feet over the pedestrian and vehicular bridges to the visitor parking lots on the Marist High School campus. The student parking lot and portions of the baseball fields will be flooded with 1 to 2 feet of water. Moderate flooding develops in residential yards downstream to Rickenbacker Drive. Some crawl spaces or foundations of homes may flood.

Minor flooding continues to expand into the woodlands and athletic fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. The water will be around two feet over the pedestrian and vehicular bridges to the visitor parking lots on the Marist High School campus. The student parking lot will be flooded and the baseball fields will begin to flood. Minor flooding of residential yards will continue downstream to Rickenbacker Drive.

Minor flooding continues of woodlands and athletic fields upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. The water will begin to flow over the pedestrian and vehicular bridges to the visitor parking lots on the Marist High School campus. The student parking lot will begin to flood. Minor flooding of residential yards will continue downstream to Rickenbacker Drive.

Flood stage is reached. Minor flooding of woodlands and athletic fields occurs upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. The water level will be hitting the pedestrian and vehicular bridges to the visitor parking lots on the Marist High School campus. This may cause some backwater and erosion eddies to form to the sides of the bridges. Minor flooding of residential yards will likely begin downstream to Rickenbacker Drive.

Bankfull conditions are reached upstream and downstream from the gage on Johnson Ferry Road. Water begins to flow into low areas of the woodlands and athletic fields near the creek. The water level will reach the bottom of the pedestrian and vehicular bridges to the visitor parking lots on the Marist High School campus.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

hydronotes.statement: Automated gauge readings are now available, courtesy of the USGS.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

value.label: Station Datum
value.description: A fixed base elevation at a tide station to which all water level measurements are referred. The datum is unique to each station and is established at a lower elevation than the water is ever expected to reach. It is referenced to the primary bench mark at the station and is held constant regardless of changes to the water level gauge or tide staff. The datum of tabulation is most often at the zero of the first tide staff installed.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
